content.situation: 两名学生在课堂上辩论跨文化团队中是否需要统一沟通风格，一方认为应适应本地习惯，另一方主张建立通用规范。
content.speakerText: I think forcing everyone to use the same communication style ignores cultural differences—doesn't that create more friction than it solves?
content.speakerTextCn: 我认为强迫所有人使用相同的沟通方式忽视了文化差异——这难道不会制造更多摩擦而不是解决问题吗？
content.responseTemplate: Actually, ______ helps teams find balance—like letting teams choose their preferred tool but training everyone on core norms.
content.hint: 强调灵活性与规范并存，避免极端做法
content.knowledgePoint: 表达平衡性观点的结构

content.situation: 在小组讨论中，一名学生质疑远程跨文化团队是否能真正实现高效协作，另一名学生试图用具体行为反驳。
content.speakerText: How can you say remote teams collaborate well? You can't read body language or build rapport through emails!
content.speakerTextCn: 你怎么能说远程团队协作得很好？你无法通过邮件读懂肢体语言或建立融洽关系！
content.responseTemplate: You're right about the challenge, but ______ like weekly video check-ins and cultural sharing circles make a real difference.
content.hint: 强调有意识设计的、重复性的互动行为

content.situation: 两位学生争论在跨文化沟通中，效率是否应优先于文化敏感度。一方认为效率更重要，另一方提出不同看法。
content.speakerText: If we prioritize cultural sensitivity, we’ll lose too much time—efficiency should come first.
content.speakerTextCn: 如果我们优先考虑文化敏感度，我们会浪费太多时间——效率应该放在第一位。
content.responseTemplate: Actually, ______ leads to better long-term efficiency—misunderstandings cost more time than slowing down to understand.
content.hint: 指出表面效率与真实效率的区别，强调理解带来的长期收益
content.knowledgePoint: 反驳效率优先论的表达结构
